originalContent
option.value:
Home Automation
Devices Pairing Automations Apps API Docs ↗
{% block content %}{% endblock %}

Home Automation Server · powered by pyatv & FastAPI

{% block scripts %}{% endblock %}

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
Home Automation
Devices Pairing Controls Automations Apps API Docs ↗
{% block content %}{% endblock %}

Home Automation Server · powered by pyatv & FastAPI

{% block scripts %}{% endblock %}

option.name: updatedContent
originalContent
option.value:
""" Home Automation Server FastAPI application factory. """
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from fastapi import FastAPI from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles from fastapi.templating import Jinja2Templates
from home_automation_server.api import devices, pairing, automations, apps, webhooks from home_automation_server.api import ui from home_automation_server.db.session import init_db from home_automation_server.core.config import settings
import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=settings.log_level) logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
@asynccontextmanager async def lifespan(application: FastAPI): """Startup / shutdown lifecycle.""" logger.info("Starting Home Automation Server…") init_db() yield logger.info("Shutting down Home Automation Server.")
def create_app() -> FastAPI: application = FastAPI( title="Home Automation Server", description="Control Apple TVs via pyatv with a FastAPI backend.", version="0.1.0", lifespan=lifespan, )
# Static files application.mount( "/static", StaticFiles(directory="home_automation_server/frontend/static"), name="static", )
# API routers application.include_router(devices.router, prefix="/devices", tags=["Devices"]) application.include_router(pairing.router, prefix="/pairing", tags=["Pairing"]) application.include_router(automations.router, prefix="/automations", tags=["Automations"]) application.include_router(apps.router, prefix="/apps", tags=["Apps"]) application.include_router(webhooks.router, prefix="/webhooks", tags=["Webhooks"])
# UI router (Jinja2 pages) application.include_router(ui.router, prefix="/ui", tags=["UI"])
@application.get("/", include_in_schema=False) async def root_redirect(): from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse return RedirectResponse(url="/ui")
return application
app = create_app()

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
""" Home Automation Server FastAPI application factory. """
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from fastapi import FastAPI from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles from fastapi.templating import Jinja2Templates
from home_automation_server.api import devices, pairing, automations, apps, webhooks, controls from home_automation_server.api import ui from home_automation_server.db.session import init_db from home_automation_server.core.config import settings
import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=settings.log_level) logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
@asynccontextmanager async def lifespan(application: FastAPI): """Startup / shutdown lifecycle.""" logger.info("Starting Home Automation Server…") init_db() yield logger.info("Shutting down Home Automation Server.")
def create_app() -> FastAPI: application = FastAPI( title="Home Automation Server", description="Control Apple TVs via pyatv with a FastAPI backend.", version="0.1.0", lifespan=lifespan, )
# Static files application.mount( "/static", StaticFiles(directory="home_automation_server/frontend/static"), name="static", )
# API routers application.include_router(devices.router, prefix="/devices", tags=["Devices"]) application.include_router(pairing.router, prefix="/pairing", tags=["Pairing"]) application.include_router(automations.router, prefix="/automations", tags=["Automations"]) application.include_router(apps.router, prefix="/apps", tags=["Apps"]) application.include_router(controls.router, prefix="/controls", tags=["Controls"]) application.include_router(webhooks.router, prefix="/webhooks", tags=["Webhooks"])
# UI router (Jinja2 pages) application.include_router(ui.router, prefix="/ui", tags=["UI"])
@application.get("/", include_in_schema=False) async def root_redirect(): from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse return RedirectResponse(url="/ui")
return application
app = create_app()
